citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
There is one bottle of gabapentin and one bottle of prednisolone (both prescribed for "Reba" (#196), a geriatric female
New Guinea singing dog at the facility) which do not have expiration dates and are stored among other, non-expired
medications in a cabinet inside the kitchen building.
Without expiration dates on containers, it cannot be determined if contents are expired or not; the use of expired
medications or medications with an unknown expiration date is not considered to be a safe or appropriate method to
prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries because expired drugs may be ineffective or produce other
undesired effects. Animals may experience discomfort or distress due to unrelieved symptoms or unexpected side effects
from the use of expired medication. The program for providing adequate veterinary care shall include the use of
appropriate methods to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries. This includes following established
procedures to ensure medication labels have expiration dates and that expired medications are removed and cannot be
administered to animals.

There are three adult wolves, "Lincoln" (#277), "Octavia" (#275) and "Merlin" (#359) housed in an enclosure with exposed
dig barrier that has spaces large enough for toes and possibly feet to pass through, posing an entrapment and injury
hazard.
Failure to construct durable facilities and maintain them can result in animal injury. The licensee must ensure that facilities
are constructed of such material and of such strength as appropriate for the animals involved; facilities must be
structurally sound and maintained in good repair to protect the animals from injury and to contain the animals.

There is an overhead beam with flaking paint and rust inside a refrigerated unit used for food storage for the animals at
the facility.
Surfaces with flaking paint and rust cannot be adequately cleaned and, when they are near or above food, can result in
food contamination. The licensee must ensure that supplies of food and bedding are stored in facilities which adequately
protect such supplies against deterioration, molding or contamination.
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class “C” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the
applicant.
All items must be in compliance within two more inspections or by 04/05/2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection.
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(2)  Repeat
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
There is one bottle of gabapentin and one bottle of prednisolone (both prescribed for "Reba" (#196), a geriatric female 
New Guinea singing dog at the facility) which do not have expiration dates and are stored among other, non-expired

Without expiration dates on containers, it cannot be determined if contents are expired or not; the use of expired

prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries because expired drugs may be ineffective or produce other 
undesired effects. Animals may experience discomfort or distress due to unrelieved symptoms or unexpected side effects

appropriate methods to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries. This includes following established 
procedures to ensure medication labels have expiration dates and that expired medications are removed and cannot be 
administered to animals. 
3.125(a)  Repeat
   
Facilities, general. 
There are three adult wolves, "Lincoln" (#277), "Octavia" (#275) and "Merlin" (#359) housed in an enclosure with exposed 
dig barrier that has spaces large enough for toes and possibly feet to pass through, posing an entrapment and injury 
hazard.  
Failure to construct durable facilities and maintain them can result in animal injury. The licensee must ensure that facilities 
are constructed of such material and of such strength as appropriate for the animals involved; facilities must be 
structurally sound and maintained in good repair to protect the animals from injury and to contain the animals. 
3.125(c)  Repeat
   
Facilities, general. 
There is an overhead beam with flaking paint and rust inside a refrigerated unit used for food storage for the animals at 
the facility.  
Surfaces with flaking paint and rust cannot be adequately cleaned and, when they are near or above food, can result in 
food contamination. The licensee must ensure that supplies of food and bedding are stored in facilities which adequately 
protect such supplies against deterioration, molding or contamination.

This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class “C” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the 
applicant. 
 
All items must be in compliance within two more inspections or by 04/05/2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee 
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection. 
 
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
